concerns.text: Two contract files with different naming conventions (hyphen vs underscore) define conflicting RegressionModel schemas. regression-model.schema.yaml uses 'vif_crossing'/'vif_braid' as separate fields while regression_model.schema.yaml uses 'vif_values' as object. Plan states 'All data files MUST conform to schema definitions in contracts/ directory' without specifying which schema is authoritative. Resolve naming inconsistency and schema divergence before implementation.

Constitution Check table marks all 7 principles as COMPLIANT without mapping specific plan elements to each constitutional requirement. Plan should include explicit annotations showing which plan sections satisfy each principle (e.g., Principle IV SSoT: 'Every figure/statistic traces back to exactly one row in data/ and one block in code/' is stated but implementation mechanism not documented). Add traceability annotations to demonstrate compliance rather than assertion.

Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) requires checksums recorded in 'state/projects/PROJ-552-quantifying-the-complexity-of-knot-diagr.yaml artifact_hashes map'. Plan mentions 'SHA-256 checksums for all data files recorded under data/ directory' with documentation in docs/reproducibility/ but does not explicitly confirm state YAML recording. Clarify whether state YAML checksum recording is implemented or if this is a gap in the plan's compliance claim.

InvariantsDataset entity in data-model defines 'validation_status' field but plan's Transformation Pipeline (Step 1-5) does not explicitly mention how this field will be populated or used. Add plan element specifying validation_status assignment logic and its relationship to SC-001 validation scope documentation.
